hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by introducing aromatic and hydrophobic substitutions at positions 5 and 11 to better engage SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current focus on ddG binding is insufficient for achieving SSTR2 selectivity. Future iterations must prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the pharmacophore core (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
Introducing charged or polar residues at these positions can disrupt conserved hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, reducing off-target binding.
